metadata.name: FERS Annuity Computation Rules
metadata.description: FERS annuity computation formulas including standard, enhanced, special category (SC), disability retirement, MRA+10 age reduction, CSRS-to-FERS transfer dual component, and congressional employee rules. Supplements rates-annual.json multipliers with detailed computation logic.

standard_formula.description: Standard FERS annuity formula for non-SC employees
standard_formula.enhanced_condition: Age 62 or older at retirement with 20 or more years of creditable service
standard_formula._note: The 1.1% enhanced multiplier applies to ALL years of service when the age/service threshold is met — it is not tiered.

special_category.description: Special Category (SC) employees receive enhanced multipliers for qualifying service
special_category.formula: (1.7% × high-3 × SC service up to 20 years) + (1.0% × high-3 × service beyond 20 years)

eligible_categories.title: Law Enforcement Officer
eligible_categories._note: Includes criminal investigators, police officers meeting LEO definition. Must be in covered LEO position.

eligible_categories.title: Nuclear Materials Courier
eligible_categories._note: Department of Energy couriers responsible for secure transport of nuclear materials.

eligible_categories.title: Customs and Border Protection Officer
eligible_categories._note: CBP Officers became eligible for SC retirement coverage effective July 6, 2008 under P.L. 110-161.

eligible_categories.title: Member of the Supreme Court Police
Air Traffic Controller
eligible_categories._note: ATCs have a separate eligibility provision (§8412(e)) but use the same 1.7%/1.0% multiplier structure.

retirement_eligibility.immediate_full: Age 50 with 20 years of SC service, or any age with 25 years of SC service
retirement_eligibility.mandatory_separation: Generally age 57 (age 56 for ATCs) — agencies must separate employees who reach mandatory retirement age with 20+ years of SC service
retirement_eligibility._note: SC eligibility and mandatory retirement ages are per 5 USC §8425.

special_category._note_enhancement_exclusion: The 1.1% enhanced multiplier (age 62+, 20+ years) does NOT apply to SC employees. SC employees use 1.7%/1.0% regardless of age.

mra_plus_10_reduction.description: Annuity reduced for early retirement under MRA+10 provision. Applies when employee has 10+ years of service and retires at MRA but does not meet unreduced retirement criteria.
mra_plus_10_reduction.reduction_basis: 5/12 of 1% for each full month the commencing date precedes the retiree's 62nd birthday
does_not_apply_if: Completed 30 or more years of creditable service
Completed 20 or more years of creditable service and annuity begins at age 60 or later

postponement.description: Employee may postpone annuity commencement to reduce or eliminate the age reduction
postponement.full_elimination: Postpone until age 62 (if less than 20 years service) or age 60 (if 20+ years service)
postponement._note: If employee postpones but begins annuity before the elimination threshold, a partial reduction still applies.

mra_plus_10_reduction.csrs_component_impact: If the retiree has a CSRS component (transferred from CSRS to FERS), the age reduction applies to BOTH the CSRS and FERS components of the annuity.

fers_disability.description: FERS disability retirement provides income replacement computed differently from standard annuity. Benefits vary by phase: first 12 months, after 12 months, and recomputation at age 62.

eligibility.condition: Must be disabled for useful and efficient service in current position due to disease or injury expected to last at least 1 year
eligibility.accommodation: Agency must certify it cannot reasonably accommodate the condition and employee has not declined reasonable reassignment offer

first_12_months.description: 60% of high-3 average salary minus 100% of any Social Security disability benefit for months entitled to SSDI
first_12_months.floor_description: If the standard FERS annuity formula (1% × high-3 × years of service) produces a higher amount, that 'earned annuity' is paid instead.
first_12_months._cola_note: COLAs are not payable during the first 12 months when receiving the 60% computation.

after_12_months.description: 40% of high-3 average salary minus 60% of any Social Security disability benefit for months entitled to SSDI
after_12_months.floor_description: If the standard FERS annuity formula produces a higher amount, that earned annuity is paid instead.

recomputation_at_62.description: At age 62, OPM recomputes the annuity as though the employee had continued working until the day before their 62nd birthday.
recomputation_at_62.service_credit: Total service includes actual FERS service plus the period of time receiving disability annuity
recomputation_at_62.high_3_adjustment: High-3 average salary is increased by all FERS COLAs received during the disability annuity period
recomputation_at_62.enhanced_condition: If actual service plus disability annuity period equals 20 or more years, the 1.1% multiplier applies to all years

earning_capacity_test.description: Disability annuitants under age 60 must report annual earnings to OPM
earning_capacity_test.restoration_threshold: Earning capacity considered restored if income from wages/self-employment equals or exceeds 80% of current salary for the position held at retirement
earning_capacity_test._note: Annuity suspended if earnings test is met or if annuitant fails to report earnings.

csrs_to_fers_transfer.description: Employees who transferred from CSRS to FERS receive a dual-component annuity computed partly under CSRS rules and partly under FERS rules.
csrs_to_fers_transfer.eligibility: At time of transfer to FERS, employee had at least 5 years of creditable civilian service covered by CSRS or Social Security (but not both). Excludes service during which partial CSRS deductions were withheld (CSRS Offset).

fers_component.description: Standard FERS formula applied to service performed after transfer to FERS
fers_component.enhanced_condition: Age 62 or older with 20 or more years of total creditable service (CSRS + FERS combined)

csrs_component.description: CSRS tiered formula applied to service performed under CSRS coverage (before transfer)
csrs_component.formula: (1.5% × high-3 × first 5 CSRS years) + (1.75% × high-3 × next 5 CSRS years) + (2.0% × high-3 × CSRS years beyond 10)

csrs_component_sc.description: CSRS formula for transferred employees who performed SC service (LEO/FF/NMC) under CSRS
csrs_component_sc.formula: (2.5% × high-3 × CSRS SC service up to 20 years) + (2.0% × high-3 × CSRS service beyond 20 years)
csrs_component_sc._note: The 2.5% rate applies to CSRS-covered LEO, firefighter, and nuclear materials courier service. Does not apply to CBP officers (who were not SC-eligible under CSRS) or to Capitol/Supreme Court Police CSRS service prior to their respective coverage dates.

csrs_to_fers_transfer.mra_plus_10_impact: If the transferred employee retires under MRA+10, the 5/12 of 1% per month age reduction applies to BOTH the CSRS and FERS components.

cola_treatment.fers_component: Receives FERS COLA (CPI-W with diet-COLA reduction for increases above 2%)

congressional_employee.description: Members of Congress and Congressional employees are eligible for enhanced FERS multipliers with 5+ years of qualifying service. Very small eligible population.
congressional_employee.formula: (1.7% × high-3 × Congressional service up to 20 years) + (1.0% × high-3 × service beyond 20 years)
congressional_employee.eligibility: 5 or more years of service as a Member of Congress and/or Congressional Employee
congressional_employee._note_capitol_police: Service as a member of the Capitol Police counts toward Congressional Employee eligibility for the enhanced multiplier under 5 USC §8415(c).

csrs_component_if_transferred.description: CSRS formula for transferred Members of Congress
csrs_component_if_transferred.first_tier_years: Years as Member of Congress and/or Congressional Employee plus qualifying military service, up to combined total
csrs_component_if_transferred.second_tier_description: Other service which, combined with first-tier service, does not exceed 10 years

congressional_employee.fers_rae_frae_exclusion: Members of Congress or Congressional Employees covered by FERS-RAE or FERS-FRAE have their annuity computed under the general FERS formula (1.0%/1.1%) and are NOT eligible for the enhanced 1.7%/1.0% Congressional calculation.
